a:T21397,<div class="specification">
<p>Hearing loss can range from mild to severe. Cochlear implants have been used in those with profound hearing loss such as children who are born deaf. The image shows a cross section of the ear with a cochlear implant.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: NIDCD (www.nidcd.nih.gov/health/cochlear-implants)]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Identify the parts of the ear labelled X and Y.</p>
<p>X:</p>
<p>Y: </p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe the use of cochlear implants in deaf patients.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/a/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>X: semicircular canals ✔</p>\n<p>Y: eardrum/tympanic membrane ✔</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>a. sound picked up by microphone relayed electronically to speech processor ✔</p>\n<p>b. speech processor filters background noise/selects only speech frequencies ✔</p>\n<p>c. «radio» signal from transmitter to receiver/stimulator which converts it to electric signal ✔</p>\n<p>d. «electrical impulses» sent to electrode «array» in cochlea<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>cochlear implant bypasses the hair cells in the cochlea ✔</p>\n<p>e. electrode/electrical signal stimulates auditory nerve «fibers in cochlea» ✔</p>\n<p>f. signals «generated by implant» sent to brain «which recognizes signals as sound» ✔</p>\n<p><em>OWTTE</em></p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Most candidates could identify the labelled parts of the ear.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Although many had a reasonable idea about how cochlear implants function, they were unable to describe this in sufficient detail. Many students talked about amplifying sound, not changing the sound into electric signals.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
